mappings.rationale: Section A.1 covers museums and galleries including museums of history and natural history, where archaeological collections are preserved and displayed.
Section B.1 specifically covers dictionaries and lexicons as systems for storage and retrieval of information.
Section B covers storage and retrieval of information including databases, electronic publishing, and the principles underlying information systems.
Section B.6 covers bibliographic and numeric databases, and big data technologies extend the techniques of information storage and retrieval.
Sections B.6-7 cover databases, magnetic and optical recordings, and electronic publishing, all relying on computer science.
Electronic systems for information storage, retrieval, and dissemination (sections B.6-7 and C.3.b) depend on computing technology.
The evolution of computing technology has transformed the institutions and techniques for information management described in this section.
Section C.3.a.ii covers newspapers and periodicals as forms of print publishing for the dissemination of knowledge.
Writing is the foundational technology for information storage and retrieval, underlying all the systems described in sections B and C.
Reading is the essential complement to publishing (C.3) and libraries (B.4) as means of knowledge dissemination.
Section C.1 references educational institutions for the advancement and dissemination of knowledge.
Section C.1 references educational institutions as key institutions for the advancement and dissemination of knowledge.
Photography is a technique for the preservation and documentation of knowledge, relevant to museums (A.1), archives (B.5), and publishing (C.3).
Section C.5 covers observatories and planetariums as institutions for the advancement and dissemination of astronomical knowledge.
Section C.5 covers observatories and planetariums, which rely on telescopes as primary instruments for knowledge advancement.
Section A.1.b.v covers museums of art and art galleries as institutions for the protection and storage of artistic objects.
Cryptography relates to the techniques for secure storage and transmission of information discussed in section B.
Surveillance technologies relate to the collection, storage, and retrieval of information discussed in section B.
Section C.2 covers academies and societies for the advancement of knowledge, which drive innovation in knowledge production.
Section C.4 covers broadcasting including television as a medium for the dissemination of knowledge.
Typography is central to print publishing (C.3.a), the selection, preparation, and marketing of printed material.
Hieroglyphs represent an ancient system of writing and information preservation, foundational to the techniques covered in this section.
Section B.7 covers magnetic and optical recordings, and C.4 covers broadcasting, both central to music technology for knowledge dissemination.
Section A.5 covers parks and nature preserves for the preservation of biological knowledge and collections.
Sections A.4 and A.5 cover zoological gardens, botanical gardens, and nature preserves that serve ecological knowledge preservation.

mappings.vsiAuthor: John A. Matthews, David T. Herbert
mappings.rationale: Section B.3 covers atlases and map collections as systems for storage and retrieval of geographic information.
Intellectual property law governs the dissemination of knowledge through publishing (C.3) and electronic media (C.3.b, C.4).
